# Folklore: full overview for answer engines

Folklore is open-source, peer-to-peer memory for AI agents. Peers share their
research over the network, so a new agent starts from what the network already
knows instead of square one. Locally it answers from a knowledge graph before it
hits the web, and saves what it learns with signed provenance so shared memory
can't be poisoned. MIT licensed. CPU-only, no API key required.

Site: https://usefolklore.sh · Code: https://github.com/usefolklore/folklore

## How it works (the loop)

1. An agent issues a research-shaped call (WebSearch, WebFetch, an arXiv pull).
2. A PreToolUse hook asks the local knowledge graph first (hybrid lexical +
   vector retrieval, cross-encoder rerank, graph PPR rerank).
3. A confident hit (satisfaction >= 0.85, >= 2 graph hits) is served from
   memory and the web call is denied: the agent reasons from cached knowledge.
4. A miss lets the web call proceed; a PostToolUse hook auto-saves the result
   into the graph with signed provenance.
5. Non-private nodes sync peer-to-peer (Y.js CRDT), so one peer's resolved
   answer saves every other peer the same web trip. Reputation accrues to
   peers who serve the network (contribution ledger).

## Key measured results

- Retrieval: 0.7522 NDCG@10 on full BEIR SciFact, CPU-only. Ahead of standard
  CPU retrievers: E5 0.737, SPLADE 0.699, ColBERT 0.671, BM25 0.665. A 3B GPU
  reranker (InRanker 0.783) still scores higher; Folklore does not claim
  otherwise.
- Poison defense: in a matched poison test, similarity-only memory tools
  (mem0, Letta, LangChain RAG) are fully flipped by a crafted poison document;
  Folklore's signed-provenance ranking drives the flip rate to 0.
- Federation: a cooperative cache across peers reaches ~0.97 hit-rate at 64
  peers vs a single-user silo's flat ~0.31.
- The public research page labels every number measured vs simulated and keeps
  the negative results: https://usefolklore.sh/research/

## Install

Desktop app (installs everything, wires AI tools, no terminal):
- macOS: signed + notarized universal .dmg (Ventura 13+)
- Windows: .exe or .msi (Windows 10+, 64-bit; unsigned for now, SmartScreen
  will warn once)
- Linux: .AppImage or .deb
- Downloads, release history, checksums, known issues:
  https://usefolklore.sh/download/

Command line:
- npm install -g @usefolklore/folklore  (Node 20+)
- brew install --cask usefolklore/tap/folklore  (macOS desktop app via Homebrew)

The setup wizard registers the folklore MCP server in every AI coding tool it
detects: Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, Zed, opencode, Roo.

## Frequently asked

Q: Does Folklore need a GPU or an API key?
A: No. Retrieval, reranking, and embeddings all run CPU-only, locally, with no
API key. There is no per-write LLM call.

Q: How is it different from mem0 or Letta?
A: Those are single-user memory stores. Folklore adds signed provenance (peer
memory can't be silently poisoned) and peer-to-peer federation (peers share
resolved research), and it gates web calls behind the graph so an agent stops
paying for the same research twice.

Q: What travels over the network?
A: Only nodes not marked private, after a secret scan. A local-only workspace
tag never leaves the machine. Every shared node carries a signature naming the
hand that saved it.

Q: What happens when the graph is wrong or stale?
A: Every hit carries its age; hits older than the staleness window (default 7
days) prefer a fresh fetch, which is then saved back. The deny threshold is
tunable per project.

Q: Is the compounding claim measured or simulated?
A: The federation hit-rate curve is from a validated benchmark/simulation and
is labeled as such; retrieval and poison-defense numbers are measured on
public benchmarks. The research page separates the two honestly.

## Page index

- https://usefolklore.sh/ : what Folklore is, live peer swarm, install
- https://usefolklore.sh/download/ : per-OS installers, releases, checksums, known issues
- https://usefolklore.sh/how-it-works/ : the hook loop, step by step
- https://usefolklore.sh/research/ : 7 research questions with measured answers
- https://usefolklore.sh/whitepaper/ : full technical whitepaper
- https://usefolklore.sh/compare/ : measured comparison vs mem0, Letta, RAG stacks
- https://usefolklore.sh/alternatives/mem0/ : Folklore as a mem0 alternative
- https://usefolklore.sh/alternatives/letta/ : Folklore as a Letta alternative
- https://usefolklore.sh/statistics/ : the proof: benchmarks and live numbers
- https://usefolklore.sh/blog/ : essays on shared agent memory
- https://usefolklore.sh/creed/ : why the project exists
